summary: chai-plugin typosquats `chai` and fetches+executes obfuscated remote JavaScript on require()
details: On `require('chai-plugin')`, lib/chai.js spawns a detached, unref'd Node child process running lib/chai/utils/assertion.js. That file is heavily obfuscated (rotated string-array decoder of obfuscator.io shape, with `_0x4a30`/`_0x495d` decoder helpers) which hides the destination URL and module names it imports. At runtime it issues an https.get, takes the response body, and evaluates it via `new Function(require, body)` invoked with `require` — arbitrary remote code execution on the installer/consumer machine, with the payload chosen at call time by whoever controls the remote endpoint. The detached, unref'd child outlives the parent process. The package name `chai-plugin` and homepage `chaiplugin.com` impersonate the legitimate `chai` assertion library, so any developer who typos the dependency name and imports the result runs the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
